description:
The Office of Congressman Maxwell Alejandro Frost (FL-10) is seeking an intern who is passionate about public service to serve in our Washington, D.C. office for the Spring 2025 semester, which runs from January 20 to March 17, 2025. This is a paid internship at $18 per hour. The hours can be flexible, but we generally require 31 hours per week.

Interns primarily focus on administrative support to meet the needs of constituents who contact the office, including answering phone calls and assisting with the coordination of tour programs. Interns will also gain experience supporting our legislative and/or communications teams based on their interests and qualifications. Interns will have a unique opportunity to gain insight into the workings of the U.S. House of Representatives and federal government while serving the people of Central Florida.

Candidates who have lived in or have ties to Florida's 10th congressional district are preferred. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Interested applicants should apply by January 7 on our website.
responsibilities: Provide administrative support to meet the needs of constituents who contact the office
Assist with the coordination of tour programs
Support legislative and/or communications teams based on interests and qualifications
Gain insight into the workings of the U.S. House of Representatives and federal government
qualifications: Available for Spring 2025 semester (January 20 to March 17, 2025)
Able to work approximately 31 hours per week
Ties to Florida's 10th congressional district preferred
Members of traditionally underrepresented communities strongly encouraged to apply
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should apply by January 7 on our website.
equal_opportunity: Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.
